principle: Split is performed at task_group_id granularity, never at session or task_instance_id granularity, to prevent leakage from near-identical parameter variants of the same template landing in different splits (Step 6.5-10).
invariants: every repeat of the same task_instance_id stays in the same split
a task_instance's normal session and its matched attack session stay in the same split
every task_instance belonging to the same task_group_id stays in the same split
near-duplicate task_instances sharing a content_bundle_id stay in the same split where possible

secondary_split.name: unseen_template_split
secondary_split.type: constraint-combination holdout, NOT a whole-family holdout
secondary_split.purpose: generalization check -- must never be summed with primary_split results (Step 6.5-12)
secondary_split.holdout_policy: test holds out task_groups whose constraint_types combination is NOT present in any primary train/validation task_group (e.g. simultaneous schedule_conflict + integration_conflict), rather than assigning entire families (e.g. all of multi_constraint_trip or hard_normal_trip) to test -- an all-hard-family holdout would create an unrealistic distribution shift and inflate normal FPR (Step 6.5-12's explicit warning).
secondary_split.finalized_in: Phase 6.5B, once actual task_instance constraint_types exist to select a genuine holdout combination

never_do: Never random-split at the session or task_instance level; never combine primary and secondary split results into one headline number.
